wins: Separation of FedEx Freight to unlock value and enhance competitiveness
Strong Q2 results with sequential improvement in DRIVE savings and adjusted operating profit
Positive outlook for December peak season with strong volume and surcharge capture
Successful implementation of DRIVE and Network 2.0 initiatives
Confidence in the separation process and continued customer service
risks: Soft industrial economy impacting B2B volumes and LTL markets
Challenging demand environment and pricing pressures
Uncertainty around the global industrial production recovery
Potential customer attrition due to business separation
Revised FY '25 adjusted EPS outlook to $19 to $20
